content: I know of no way to do that. However, if your goal is to make the target of the URL more descriptive or obvious, and you're composing a message in HTML format, you could use the "Add Link" button which will let you add a shorter description of the URL. See the attached image.

content:
paulajvannoy said
I had a problem and deleted thunderbird. Now I have reinstalled and cant get to the allow button for account setup. It seems to be way down the page and I cant sroll down to get to it.

Some programs don't always fit well on a small screen, like a laptop's. Fortunately, the content of most Thunderbird windows can be shrunk with the same keystrokes you can use to shrink a web page content. You can temporarily shrink it until the button is accessible.

<Crtl+plus> to enlarge <Ctrl+minus> to shrink <Ctrl+0> to return to default size
creator: rednoise

content:
Check the setting in <Edit | Settings | Composition | Addressing> and enable the "When addressing messages, look for matching entries in: Local Address Books" option.

Also, when you're in the Write window, you can toggle the "Contacts" button to show a mini-address book - select an entry and double-click or click the "Add to" an entry to add it to the message.

see the screen shots below.
creator: rednoise

content: Where are your contacts stored? Are they in Google Contacts? If so, open the Address Book, click on the little icon to the left of "New Contact" and choose "Add CardDAV Address Book". Enter your Gmail credentials if prompted. Otherwise, you may have to export them from wherever they are now stored, transfer them to the laptop, and import them into Thunderbird. If you have a choice of export formats, choose VCF (vCard) format - it's much simpler and less error-prone.
creator: rednoise
